definitions: To gently press and twist an ingredient (fruit, herbs, sugar) in the bottom of a mixing glass or shaker to release its flavor and oils, without pulverizing it.
An informal unit of measure — a single quick shake of a bitters bottle, roughly 0.92 ml.
By extension, any very small, imprecise addition of an ingredient.
Shaking a cocktail (typically one containing egg white) without ice first, to build texture and foam, followed by a second shake with ice to chill and dilute.
A sweet, cloudy syrup made from almonds, sugar, and often orange-flower water — a foundational ingredient in tiki cocktails such as the Mai Tai.
An orange-peel-flavored liqueur, historically made from the dried peel of the laraha citrus grown on the island of Curaçao.
